Method provenance block:
Property: Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰), dual descriptor, and most reactive atoms of formaldehyde
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, RI density fitting) in PySCF 2.x
Geometry provenance: fixed experimental geometry supplied by the user (file “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz”); no re-optimization – single-point N, N + 1, and N – 1 charge states computed at the same B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (DFT//fixed geometry)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (N), cation doublet (+1, 2 S + 1 = 2) and anion doublet (–1, 2 S + 1 = 2) for Fukui differences
Software & version: chemkit “fukui-reactivity” skill backed by PySCF DFT (RI enabled)
Convergence & caveats: all three SCF calculations reported convergence; integrity.status = "ok" with trustworthy = true. The engine issued three warnings (verbatim below) concerning Fukui charge conservation checks and schema header; see warnings list. No other warnings.
